chapter_title: Licenses Issued by California Highway Patrol
text: All accusations against licensees shall be filed within three years after the act or omission alleged as the ground for disciplinary action, except that with respect to an accusation alleging a violation of subdivision (f) of Section 2542, the accusation may be filed within two years after the discovery by the California Highway Patrol of the alleged facts constituting the fraud or misrepresentation prohibited by said section.
markdown:
# Vehicle Code
## Division 2. ADMINISTRATION [1500 - 3093]
### Chapter 2.5. Licenses Issued by California Highway Patrol [2500 - 2574]
#### Article 5. Denial, Suspension and Revocation [2540 - 2549]
##### § 2547

All accusations against licensees shall be filed within three years after the act or omission alleged as the ground for disciplinary action, except that with respect to an accusation alleging a violation of subdivision (f) of Section 2542, the accusation may be filed within two years after the discovery by the California Highway Patrol of the alleged facts constituting the fraud or misrepresentation prohibited by said section.

*(Added by Stats. 1968, Ch. 1309.)*

subsections.text: All accusations against licensees shall be filed within three years after the act or omission alleged as the ground for disciplinary action, except that with respect to an accusation alleging a violation of subdivision (f) of Section 2542, the accusation may be filed within two years after the discovery by the California Highway Patrol of the alleged facts constituting the fraud or misrepresentation prohibited by said section.
